docs.title: Research Group Wiki
docs.text:
Welcome to our group's knowledge base. Use the navigation tabs above or the cards below to get started.

  • Progress

    Current status of ongoing problems, open tasks, and assignments across the group.

    View progress

  • Findings

    Documented results, theorems, proof sketches, and key insights we have established.

    Browse findings

  • Papers

    Shared reading list with status, venue, and notes for every paper we are using.

    View papers

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Clone the repo and install dependencies (see the README).
  2. Edit or create a .md file inside docs/.
  3. Push to main — the site rebuilds and deploys automatically in ~1–2 minutes.

Quick edits without cloning

Every page has a :material-pencil: edit button (top right) that opens the file directly in GitHub's web editor.

docs.title: Findings
docs.text: Document theorems, lemmas, proof sketches, conjectures, and other insights here. Each entry should be self-contained enough that someone unfamiliar with the day-to-day context can understand what was shown, how, and why it matters.

docs.title: [Result Title — e.g., "Reduction from Set Disjointness"]
docs.text:
Date established: YYYY-MM-DD Established by: Name(s) Related problem: Progress entry or external reference

Statement:

Theorem 1

Let \( G = (V, E) \) be a graph on \( n \) vertices. Then [state the result formally here].

\[ T(n) \geq \Omega\!\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right) \]

Proof sketch:

Describe the key idea — the construction, the reduction, or the key inequality — without necessarily being fully rigorous. Link to a paper or note for full details.

  1. First step of the argument.
  2. Second step, possibly with inline math: we apply the bound \( |S| \leq k \) to conclude...
  3. Conclude.

Open questions / follow-ups:

  • Does the bound extend to the multi-pass setting?
  • Can we tighten the constant?

docs.title: [Conjecture Title]
docs.text:
Proposed by: Name Date: YYYY-MM-DD

Statement:

Conjecture 1

[State the conjecture formally. E.g.:]

For every \( \varepsilon > 0 \), no algorithm using \( O(n^{1-\varepsilon}) \) space can \( (1+\delta) \)-approximate [problem] for \( \delta < 1/2 \).

Evidence / motivation: Why do we believe this? What partial results or intuition support it?

docs.title: How to Add a New Finding
docs.text:
Copy the template below and fill it in. For formal proofs, a proof sketch here plus a link to a PDF or Overleaf document is usually the right level of detail.

### [Result Title]

**Date established:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Established by:** Name(s)
**Related problem:** [link](../progress/index.md)

**Statement:**

!!! note "Theorem N"
    [Formal statement with LaTeX as needed]

**Proof sketch:**

> Key idea in 1–3 sentences.

1. ...
2. ...
3. ...

**Open questions / follow-ups:**

- [ ] ...

docs.title: Papers
docs.text:
All papers the group has read, is reading, or plans to read. Keep the table updated as you work through papers. Add notes inline or link to a longer write-up in Findings.

Status key:

Badge Meaning To Read On our list but not yet started Reading Actively being read by someone Read Finished (and summarised?) Referenced Cited in our work / used as background

docs.title: Reading List
docs.text: Title Authors Year Status Notes Online Graph Edge-Coloring in the Random-Order Arrival Model Bahmani, Mehta, Motwani 2012 To Read Try to see if we can help solve Conjecture 3.12 aristotle aristotle N/A To Read Formal Reasoning Agent SHORT PROOFS IN COMBINATORICS, PROBABILITY AND NUMBER THEORY II Alexeev, et al. 2026 Reading Collection of Erdos Problems we are considering to help solve Solving an Open Problem in Theoretical Physics using AI-Assisted Discovery Brenner, et al. 2026 To Read Agent math in physics ScaleAutoResearch-Ramsey ypwang61 2026 To Read GitHub repo having used agents to improve lower bounds for Ramsey Numbers AlphaEvolve Google 2026 Reading Google's AI Agent currently helping to make progress in algorithm discovery and optimization Towards Solving the Gilbert-Pollak Conjecture via Large Language Models Ke, Huang, Shu, et al. 2026 To Read Open problem in combinatorial optimization and computational geometry An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry OpenAI 2026 Reading Example of AI Agent working successfully towards impactful result Majority-of-Three: The Simplest Optimal Learner? Kasper Green Larsen, et al. 2024 To Read Read this and also look at other papers by Kasper Green Larsen A 4.509-Approximation Algorithm for Generalized Min Sum Set Cover Bhangale and Zhang 2026 Reading Example application in approximation algorithms A 1.488-approximation algorithm for the uncapacitated facility location problem Shi Li 2013 Potential application in another approximation problem Proofs: Checking vs Finding Sanjeev Arora 2026 Read Talk by Dr. Arora Can AI Help with Mathematics and Computer Research? Prabhakar Raghavan 2026 Read Talk by Dr. Raghavan

docs.title: How to Add a Paper
docs.text:
Add a new row to the table above:

| [Title](URL) | Authors | Year | Status | Notes |

Where URL is the arXiv link, DOI, or PDF URL.

docs.title: Useful Search Resources
docs.text:
• arXiv cs.CC / cs.DS / math.CO — preprints in complexity, algorithms, combinatorics
  • Semantic Scholar — citation search and "related papers" graph
  • Connected Papers — visualise the citation neighbourhood of a paper
  • ECCC — Electronic Colloquium on Computational Complexity
  • DBLP — comprehensive CS bibliography

docs.title: Research Progress
docs.text: Track the current state of all ongoing work here. Each task or problem gets its own coloured block indicating its status. Update your blocks whenever you make progress or hit a blocker — and move completed items to the Completed section.

docs.title: Active Work
docs.text:
Problem: [Your Problem Title Here]

Assigned to: Alice, Bob Started: YYYY-MM-DD Status: 🔄 In Progress

Description: A concise statement of the problem being worked on.

Current approach: What technique or reduction you are attempting, and why you believe it could work.

Blockers: None currently. (Or describe what is blocking progress.)

Problem: [A Blocked Problem]

Assigned to: Charlie Started: YYYY-MM-DD Status: 🚫 Blocked

Description: A concise statement of the problem.

Current approach: Approach being attempted.

Blockers: Describe specifically what is blocking progress and what help or insight is needed.

docs.title: Completed
docs.text:
Task: [A Completed Task]

Completed by: Alice Date: YYYY-MM-DD

Brief summary of what was accomplished and any relevant links (e.g., to a Finding or Paper).

docs.title: How to Add a New Entry
docs.text:
Copy one of the three templates below into the appropriate section above.

In Progress — use !!! warning

!!! warning "Problem: YOUR TITLE"
    **Assigned to:** Name(s)
    **Started:** YYYY-MM-DD
    **Status:** 🔄 In Progress

    **Description:**
    ...

    **Current approach:**
    ...

    **Blockers:**
    None / describe blocker.

Blocked — use !!! danger

!!! danger "Problem: YOUR TITLE"
    **Assigned to:** Name(s)
    **Started:** YYYY-MM-DD
    **Status:** 🚫 Blocked

    **Description:**
    ...

    **Current approach:**
    ...

    **Blockers:**
    Describe the blocker.

Completed — use !!! success

!!! success "Task: YOUR TITLE"
    **Completed by:** Name
    **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD

    Summary of outcome and any links.
